q: A wave transfers:
opts: Energy without net transfer of matter
exp: Waves carry energy while particles oscillate about mean position.

q: The SI unit of frequency is:
Wave velocity is given by:
The distance between two consecutive crests is:
exp: One complete spatial cycle is wavelength.

q: Frequency is the reciprocal of:
Which wave requires material medium for propagation?
Light waves are:
exp: Light consists of transverse electric and magnetic oscillations.

q: In transverse waves, particles vibrate:
In longitudinal waves, compressions are regions of:
The unit of wavelength is:
If frequency doubles and wave speed remains constant, wavelength becomes:
exp: v = fλ, so λ inversely proportional to frequency.

q: A wave having frequency 50 Hz and wavelength 4 m travels with speed:
Energy carried by a wave is proportional to:
exp: Wave energy depends on square of amplitude.

q: A ripple in water is an example of:
The number of oscillations per second is:

Sound cannot travel through:
Wave motion transfers:
exp: Primary function of waves is energy transfer.

q: The phase difference between two points separated by one wavelength is:
exp: One complete cycle corresponds to 2π phase.

q: A crest is a point of:
exp: Crest is highest point in transverse wave.

q: The time taken to complete one oscillation is:
exp: Time period is duration of one cycle.

q: If wavelength increases at constant frequency, wave speed:
Ultrasonic waves have frequency:
Which is NOT a characteristic of wave motion?
exp: Particles oscillate about mean position only.

q: In a longitudinal wave, distance between two compressions is:
exp: Successive compressions are one wavelength apart.

q: A standing wave is formed due to:
opts: Interference of two identical waves moving opposite
exp: Superposition of opposite traveling waves forms standing wave.

q: Nodes in stationary waves are points of:
Antinodes are points of:
Distance between node and adjacent antinode is:
The phase difference between adjacent nodes is:
exp: Successive loops are out of phase.

q: The Doppler effect occurs due to:
opts: Relative motion between source and observer
exp: Apparent frequency changes due to relative motion.

q: If source approaches observer, observed frequency:
Echo is due to:
exp: Sound reflects back from distant obstacle.

q: Audible sound range for humans is approximately:
exp: Normal hearing range is 20 Hz to 20 kHz.

Which property determines pitch of sound?
Loudness of sound depends mainly on:
Beats are produced due to interference of two waves having:
exp: Frequency difference causes periodic variation in intensity.

q: Beat frequency equals:
The principle of superposition applies to:
A wave pulse reflected from fixed end undergoes:
exp: Reflection at fixed end causes phase change of π.

q: A tuning fork of frequency 256 Hz completes how many vibrations in 2 seconds?
The equation y = A sin(ωt − kx) represents:
exp: This is equation of traveling wave.

q: Angular frequency ω is related to frequency f by:
Wave number k is given by:
exp: Wave number measures phase change per unit distance.

q: Mechanical waves cannot propagate in vacuum because:
exp: Medium particles are required to transfer disturbance.
